answer:
\boxed{A}

The correct translation of "Not all lobsters are red" is best represented by negating the universal statement that all lobsters are red, i.e., "All lobsters are red" translates to \((\forall x)(Lx \supset Rx)\), and its negation is \(~( \forall x)(Lx \supset Rx)\). This matches option A.
